validation_question: Did the upstream/transcript-5′ native locus join correctly to the synthetic linker/ISC1 module?
primer_placement_logic: One locus-specific primer outside the 5′ homology arm, ideally in ChrVII:427170..427569; one internal primer inside linker/early ISC1 module, roughly donor bp 560–780.
coordinate_basis: 5′ arm donor bp 1–500 maps to ChrVII:426670..427169; anchor is donor bp 366–500; 5′ junction is donor bp 500/501.
expected_edited_result: Single edited-locus band only if the cassette integrated at the correct 5′ junction.
expected_wt_result: No product with the internal ISC1/linker primer because WT lacks the synthetic module.
expected_band_size_logic: Band ≈ external-primer distance outside 5′ arm + 500 bp 5′ arm + internal-primer distance into donor module. With a 150–350 bp external offset and a 60–280 bp internal offset, expect roughly 710–1,130 bp.
sanger_or_readout_target: Sequence across native-anchor terminus → (GGGGS)2 linker → early ISC1 payload.
pass_fail_interpretation: PASS if one clean product sequences across the 5′ arm/linker junction with no duplicated anchor and no indels at the junction.
caveats_controls: Requires a primer outside the donor sequence; a primer inside the 5′ arm alone would also amplify donor template and is not locus-diagnostic.

validation_question: Did the synthetic ISC1/TAA module join correctly to the downstream/transcript-3′ native locus?
primer_placement_logic: One internal primer in late ISC1/TAA-side module, roughly donor bp 1550–1796; one locus-specific primer outside the 3′ homology arm, ideally in ChrVII:424462..424861.
coordinate_basis: ISC1+TAA is donor bp 531–1799; 3′ arm donor bp 1800–2299 maps to ChrVII:424862..425361; 3′ junction is donor bp 1799/1800.
expected_edited_result: Single edited-locus band only if the cassette integrated at the correct 3′ junction.
expected_wt_result: No product with the internal ISC1 primer because WT lacks the synthetic module.
expected_band_size_logic: Band ≈ distance from internal module primer to donor bp 2299 + external-primer distance outside 3′ arm. With a late-payload primer and 150–350 bp external offset, target roughly 700–1,100 bp.
sanger_or_readout_target: Sequence across late ISC1 payload → terminal TAA → start of 3′ homology arm/native downstream locus.
pass_fail_interpretation: PASS if one clean product sequences across the payload/TAA/3′ arm boundary with the expected terminal stop and no indels at the junction.
caveats_controls: The internal primer should sit upstream of the 3′ arm; otherwise the assay may not prove module-to-locus integration.

validation_question: Is the native catalytic region that should be replaced absent from the edited clone?
primer_placement_logic: Design both primers inside the native deleted region ChrVII:425362..426669, avoiding any retained anchor sequence and avoiding the homology arms.
coordinate_basis: Deleted native region is ChrVII:425362..426669 (1308 bp); replacement module is 1,299 bp; net chromosome change is -9 bp.
expected_edited_result: No band in a pure edited haploid clone; a band suggests WT carryover, incomplete editing, mixed colony, or contamination.
expected_wt_result: WT positive-control band, ideally 300–800 bp selected from inside the deleted native catalytic region.
expected_band_size_logic: Choose a 300–800 bp internal WT amplicon. Do not rely only on outside-arm full-locus PCR because OCH1 differs by only 9 bp from WT and may not resolve cleanly on a standard gel.
sanger_or_readout_target: Usually gel/readout only; Sanger optional if the WT band appears and identity needs confirmation.
pass_fail_interpretation: PASS if edited clone has junction-positive bands and no WT-deleted-region band, while WT control gives the expected band.
caveats_controls: Include WT genomic DNA as positive control and no-template control; colony purity matters.

validation_question: Is the intended ISC1α/β/γ payload present within the edited locus?
primer_placement_logic: Design primer pair entirely inside donor bp 531–1796, preferably spanning a variant-specific/diversified segment if available; avoid homology arms.
coordinate_basis: Payload coding region without stop is donor bp 531–1796; payload+TAA is donor bp 531–1799 for ISC1α / alpha.
expected_edited_result: Positive internal ISC1 band, target 500–900 bp.
expected_wt_result: No band at this locus-specific assay because WT OCH1/MNN2/MNN5 lacks the inserted ISC1 payload.
expected_band_size_logic: Band equals selected internal payload primer distance, usually 500–900 bp for clean amplification and optional Sanger.
sanger_or_readout_target: Optional Sanger of internal payload segment; full 1,266 bp payload requires overlapping reads, not one read.
pass_fail_interpretation: PASS if payload band is present along with both junction-positive PCRs.
caveats_controls: Not sufficient alone: donor plasmid/synthesis-template contamination could amplify. Interpret only with junction PCRs.

validation_question: Does sequence-level evidence confirm the exact engineered junctions and coding-frame logic?
primer_placement_logic: Sequence the 5′ junction PCR product, the 3′ junction PCR product, and one or more internal payload PCR products if full payload identity is required.
coordinate_basis: Critical donor junctions: 5′ arm/linker at 500/501; linker/payload at 530/531; payload/TAA at 1796/1797; TAA/3′ arm at 1799/1800; retained anchor at donor bp 366–500.
expected_edited_result: Trace should match donor architecture: retained native anchor inside 5′ arm, then linker, ISC1 payload, single terminal TAA, then 3′ arm.
expected_wt_result: WT sample should not produce junction amplicons with internal ISC1 primers.
expected_band_size_logic: Use PCR products sized for clean Sanger reads, ideally ~700–1,100 bp with the junction within the first ~200–500 bp from a sequencing primer.
sanger_or_readout_target: 5′ read: anchor → linker → early ISC1. 3′ read: late ISC1 → TAA → 3′ arm. Internal reads: overlapping payload coverage if needed.
pass_fail_interpretation: PASS if chromatograms are clean and align exactly to v1.0 donor coordinates with no anchor duplication, no frameshift, and no internal premature stop in the intended ORF.
caveats_controls: This is still computational/wet-lab validation planning. Actual validation requires primer design, PCR, cleanup, Sanger submission, and alignment.
